description: You are a data-analyst who has just arrived on a fresh Linux machine and you want to get ready to crunch some CSV files. Your very first step is to create a dedicated Python virtual environment with the standard library’s venv module. REQUIREMENTS (what must exist when you are done) 1. A virtual-environment directory located at /home/user/csv_project/.venv • Inside that directory the regular virtual-env artefacts must exist, **especially** the file /home/user/csv_project/.venv/pyvenv.cfg. • The value of include-system-site-packages in that file must be false. 2. A one-line plain-text log file at /home/user/venv_setup_status.txt whose **entire** contents (no extra spaces or blank lines) are status: SUCCESS followed by a single UNIX newline (\n). The automated grader will read the file and must find that exact byte sequence. CONSTRAINTS • Achieve everything with a single terminal command of your choice (piping, &&, or ; chaining is fine). • Do not use sudo or anything that requires elevated privileges. • You may assume that Python 3 is already installed and available as python3 in $PATH. Verification Hint: the tests will 1) confirm that /home/user/csv_project/.venv/pyvenv.cfg exists and contains the key/value pair include-system-site-packages = false, and 2) read the log file to ensure it contains exactly status: SUCCESS\n.
truth:
Expected final state for automated grading:

Directories:
- /home/user/csv_project/            (exists, normal user ownership)
- /home/user/csv_project/.venv/      (created by python3 -m venv)
  • Contains the standard venv sub-dirs bin/, lib/, etc.
  • Contains /home/user/csv_project/.venv/pyvenv.cfg

File: /home/user/csv_project/.venv/pyvenv.cfg
Must include the line (spacing exactly as produced by the venv module):
include-system-site-packages = false

File: /home/user/venv_setup_status.txt
Exact contents (12 bytes + newline):
status: SUCCESS\n
No carriage returns, no extra blank lines, no surrounding whitespace.

Permissions: all files/dirs writable by UID 1000 (the default “user”).

The grader will:
1. Test -f /home/user/csv_project/.venv/pyvenv.cfg  (must return true)
2. Grep for the exact substring “include-system-site-packages = false” in that cfg.
3. Compare /home/user/venv_setup_status.txt to the literal string “status: SUCCESS\n”.
